title: Pellionia fruticulosa
intro: Pellionia fruticulosa, also known as Pellionia, is a type of tropical plant belonging to the family Urticaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, Pellionia is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Pellionia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal preparations, Pellionia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
taxonomy: Pellionia fruticulosa is classified within the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family consists of flowering plants that are often vines or shrubs. The genus Pellionia, to which Pellionia fruticulosa belongs, is native to tropical regions of Asia. Pellionia fruticulosa specifically is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia.
